summary: Full end-to-end verification of the Email Template & Campaign System (Iteration 28). Backend: 24/24 pytest tests passed covering 7 default templates listing/get/update/reset, custom template create/delete (default delete returns 400 as expected), template preview rendering, email queue + stats (scheduler_running=true, batch_size=50, batch_interval_mins=5), and campaign send that queues emails. Frontend: All 3 tabs (Templates/Queue/Email Logs) present with counts; all 7 template cards rendered with Edit/Preview buttons; Edit modal shows data-testid='rich-text-toolbar' + subject/body inputs; Preview modal renders sample data (Dr. John Doe, IDSEA) with no {{ placeholders remaining; Queue tab shows 4 stat cards (Pending=11, Sent=0, Failed=0, Processing=0) plus 'Scheduler: Running' and 'Batch: 50 emails / 5 min'; Create Template and Send Campaign modals expose all required input fields with proper data-testids.
critical_code_review_comments: EmailTemplatesAdmin.jsx is 564 lines - approaching maintainability limit; consider extracting the three modals (Edit, Create, Campaign, Preview) into separate sub-components.
Backend server.py is ~4527 lines - significantly beyond 700-line guideline; should be split into feature modules (templates, queue, campaigns, members, events, etc.). Non-blocking but worth planning.

process_email_queue scheduler catches exceptions and re-loops every EMAIL_BATCH_INTERVAL but doesn't reset _email_scheduler_running on unhandled cancellation; acceptable for now but consider setting it False in a finally block.

Campaign send endpoint doesn't validate the template_key exists before queueing - if an invalid key is passed the queue will accumulate unsendable items. Consider a pre-check against DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATES or db.email_templates.

recipient_group filter only supports academic/entrepreneur/corporate/all_members - if 'student' or other membership types exist they'd be silently included in 'all_members' only. Fine given the current data model.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted one custom template with prefix 'test_custom_' during pytest run. One TEST campaign was queued with ~11 pending emails - SMTP is configured with test.com hostnames so no real emails were delivered; these will accumulate as pending or transition to failed on next scheduler tick (harmless in preview env).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email queue currently has 11 pending items from this test run using non-functional SMTP (smtp.test.com). If needed, call POST /api/admin/email-queue/clear-sent (no-op for pending) or manually delete from email_queue collection to reset. All backend contracts confirmed. Frontend Escape key does NOT close modals - modals use their own X or Cancel button; if writing new UI tests, reload the page between modal-based sub-tests OR click Cancel/X explicitly (do not rely on Escape).
